title: Redo settings pane UI without NavigationSplitView workaround
description: Replace the settings/project settings NavigationSplitView implementation with a macOS 14-compatible pane selector that matches standard settings behavior, remove the AppKit toolbar cleanup bridge, and verify build/visual output.
close_reason: Implemented fixed Xcode-style settings chrome with no collapse affordance, plus grouped Back/Forward pane history; build, verify launch, visual inspection, and tests passed.

comments.author: Beadazzle
comments.text: Replaced the adaptive TabView/sidebar implementation with a fixed SettingsPaneContainer. The delayed collapse glyph was owned by SwiftUI's internal sidebar style, so the final UI keeps the Xcode-style vertical pane list while applying system glass/material directly to the pane surface. Verified after a 1s wait in the live Settings window: no Hide Sidebar toolbar item, splitter, or collapse control. Also ran rtk swift build, rtk swift test, and rtk ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify.

comments.author: Beadazzle
comments.text: Follow-up UI pass: moved the settings content into the titlebar region, added an Xcode-style grouped Back/Forward control with pane-history state, tightened the detail leading offset, and kept the fixed sidebar surface so SwiftUI cannot inject the delayed collapse affordance. Verified live window after delay and checked Back becomes enabled after selecting UI. rtk swift test passed: 121 tests, 0 failures.

title: Redo Settings UI and harden project settings MVP
close_reason: Implemented native Settings redo and hardened project settings behavior

title: Implement Xcode-style settings MVP
description: Replace Beadazzle settings with an Xcode-style macOS settings window, add project settings for type/status visibility and custom workflow values, persist preferences, and wire stale cutoff plus bd CLI path configuration.
acceptance_criteria: App settings use vertical panes for General and UI. Project settings open from the project switcher ellipsis. Type/status visibility is per-project, custom add/delete uses bd config, hidden values remain valid when used by existing beads, and tests/build/launch verification pass.
close_reason: Implemented Xcode-style settings MVP and verified with swift test/build/app launch

title: Document public build, run, and contributor setup
description: Update contributor-facing docs so someone new to Beadazzle can build and run the app without Codex-specific or local-only assumptions.
acceptance_criteria: README documents requirements, bd installation or BEADAZZLE_BD_PATH, swift build, swift test, script/build_and_run.sh, generated output directories, and troubleshooting for common first-run issues.

title: Add GitHub Actions CI for Swift build and tests
description: Add a macOS GitHub Actions workflow so public contributors and maintainers get a repeatable build and test signal on pushes and pull requests.
acceptance_criteria: A workflow under .github/workflows runs swift build and swift test on macOS; CI passes on the default branch; README optionally shows the build status badge.

title: Add third-party notices for packaged dependencies
description: Review direct and transitive SwiftPM dependencies plus bundled assets before distributing source or binaries. Current pins include swift-markdown-engine, HighlighterSwift, SwiftMath, and bundled font or theme assets.
acceptance_criteria: THIRD_PARTY_NOTICES.md or equivalent exists; Package.resolved dependencies are listed with licenses; bundled fonts and syntax theme assets are covered; README or release notes point users to the notices.

title: Add open-source governance files
description: Add the repository files external users expect before a public release: an explicit open-source license, contribution guidance, security reporting guidance, and lightweight issue or PR templates if useful.
acceptance_criteria: A LICENSE file is present; CONTRIBUTING.md explains how to build, test, and contribute; SECURITY.md explains vulnerability reporting; README links to these files where appropriate.

title: Prepare Beadazzle for open-source release
description: Track the concrete project hygiene, legal, CI, documentation, and distribution work needed before making Beadazzle public.
acceptance_criteria: Open-source release readiness work is represented by child beads, and the repo has enough documentation and automation for external contributors to build, test, and understand release expectations.

title: Harden bd command discovery and metadata parsing
description: Resolve bd from public-safe locations, support GUI PATH limitations, and parse metadata JSON from stdout without stderr contamination.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with SwiftPM tests, SwiftPM build, launch verification, and Xcode MCP build/tests.

title: Make bookmark presets project-semantic safe
description: Keep the existing preset bookmarks, but derive them from Beads status metadata without hard-coded custom user or project semantics.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with SwiftPM tests, SwiftPM build, launch verification, and Xcode MCP build/tests.

title: Fix new bead create status behavior
description: New bead editor exposed status, but bd create cannot accept status directly. Hide status on create and keep status updates on existing beads only.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with SwiftPM tests, SwiftPM build, launch verification, and Xcode MCP build/tests.

title: Optimistic in-memory mutations instead of full re-export + reparse + rebuild
description: Every mutation (save/close/bulkSet/delete/dependency/comment) calls refresh(reason:.mutation) which re-exports JSONL, reparses the whole file, and rebuilds the entire BeadProjectIndex for a one-field edit (BeadStore.swift ~838/893/912/929). The rebuild is off-main so it does not freeze the UI, but it adds write->export->file-watch->reload latency Linear avoids by patching in memory. Explore optimistic in-memory patching of the index for single-issue edits while still writing through bd for correctness. Largest change; touches the 'always go through bd' invariant.

title: Background search filtering / add cheap searchable index
description: Search filtering runs localizedStandardContains over all candidate IDs synchronously on the @MainActor (BeadProjectIndex.filteredIssueIDs, ~line 247-249). It is debounced 140ms but at Linear scale (5k-50k issues) each keystroke is an O(n) locale-aware scan on the main thread. Once the query pipeline is backgrounded this rides along; additionally consider precomputing a lowercased searchable string per issue and using plain contains for the common case, falling back to localizedStandardContains only when needed.
close_reason: Added a cheap searchable index. BeadProjectIndex now precomputes a folded (case/diacritic/width-insensitive, id-prefixed) searchable string per issue at build time (foldedSearchTextByID + foldedForSearch helper). Filtering folds the query once and uses a plain String.contains instead of running localizedStandardContains per candidate per keystroke — documented-equivalent semantics, but off the locale-aware path. The query pipeline was already moved off the main actor in e004ec9, so this rides along there. Added testSearchIsCaseAndDiacriticInsensitive; full suite green (122 tests).

title: Evaluate hig-doctor for settings UI audit
description: Try raintree-technology/hig-doctor against Beadazzle, summarize useful findings, and decide whether it should inform the settings UI redo.
close_reason: hig-doctor trial completed; results summarized in bead comment

comments.author: Beadazzle
comments.text: Ran hig-doctor from /private/tmp/hig-doctor against Beadazzle. JSON audit scanned 71 SwiftUI files, found 0 critical, 0 serious, 51 moderate concerns, 268 positive patterns. Detailed output flagged SettingsView and ProjectSettingsView NavigationSplitView usage under Layout & Navigation; references reinforce macOS settings should use expected Settings entry point, noncustomizable always-visible pane toolbar, active-pane indication, title updates, and last-pane restoration. Conclusion: useful as lint/reference input, not sufficient as final visual authority.

title: Implement responsive detail layout ribbon
description: Implement the approved Linear-style responsive Beadazzle detail layout: hide the issue list on narrower widths, replace the compact stacked inspector with a metadata ribbon under breadcrumbs, and keep wide inspector behavior unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: At compact detail widths, metadata renders as a horizontal editable ribbon below breadcrumbs; the issue list hides below the planned breakpoint; project breadcrumb returns to the list; wide inspector rail remains unchanged; layout tests and Swift build/test verification pass.
notes:
Implementation started in Codex. Plan: refactor metadata controls, add compact detail ribbon, collapse issue list at narrower widths, update layout tests, and verify with Swift test/build.
Implemented responsive detail layout. Verification passed: rtk swift test, rtk swift build, and rtk ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify. Note: initial sandboxed Swift commands failed on user cache writes; reruns with approved cache permissions succeeded.
Addressing review findings: reserved list max width in detail breakpoints, changed ribbon chips to min-height sizing, and split metadata controls into focused SwiftUI view files before rerunning verification.
Review fixes verified: rtk git diff --check, rtk swift test, rtk swift build, and rtk ./script/build_and_run.sh --verify all passed after tightening breakpoints and splitting metadata views.
close_reason: Responsive detail layout implementation and review follow-ups are complete. Verified with git diff check, Swift tests, Swift build, and launch verification.

title: Select newly created bead after inline create
description: After saving a new bead from the inline creation flow, capture the created bead ID, refresh the store, and reveal/select the created bead instead of returning to the empty detail state.
acceptance_criteria: Creating via toolbar checkmark and Cmd-S selects the new bead after refresh. The issue list reveals the created bead when filters/search would otherwise hide it. Create failures keep the draft open and show the existing error alert. Command/service behavior is covered by focused tests.

title: Document data access and privacy stance
description: Document what Beadazzle reads and writes in a user repository, how bd is invoked for mutations, and whether any telemetry or diagnostic logging exists by default.
acceptance_criteria: README, SECURITY.md, or a dedicated privacy note states that Beadazzle reads .beads data, routes writes through bd, does not enable telemetry by default, and avoids logging issue contents except for explicit diagnostics.

title: Decide source-only versus signed binary release path
description: Decide whether the first public release only publishes source or also ships a downloadable macOS app bundle. If binaries are shipped, define the signing, hardened runtime, notarization, and validation workflow.
acceptance_criteria: The release strategy is documented; source-only release requirements are clear; if binary distribution is selected, the Developer ID signing, hardened runtime, notarization, and stapling validation steps are captured.

title: Add focused coverage for Beadazzle data paths
description: Add behavioral tests for command construction, metadata parsing, index filtering, and large dataset behavior without fragile UI text assertions.
close_reason: Implemented and validated with SwiftPM tests, SwiftPM build, launch verification, and Xcode MCP build/tests.

title: Add os_signpost instrumentation for load/filter/sort/row-build
description: Add os_signpost intervals around BeadProjectLoader.loadProject (parse + index build), BeadStore.applyFilters, BeadIssueListQuery.sortedIssueIDs, and issueListRows so we can measure real timings at scale with Instruments and justify further optimization. Measurement to back the perf work in the sibling performance beads.
close_reason: Added os_signpost intervals: PerformanceSignposts helper (Support/PerformanceSignposts.swift) with load + query OSSignposters. Instrumented SnapshotRead + IndexBuild in BeadProjectLoader, and Filter/Sort/RowBuild in BeadIssueListQuery.

title: Extract shared detail editing page shell
description: IssueDetailPage and IssueCreationPage share the same toolbar-divider-geometry-scroll layout and responsive padding logic. Extract a small shared shell only if it keeps the detail/create flows readable and avoids future drift.
acceptance_criteria: Detail and creation pages still match current behavior across compact and inspector-rail layouts. The shared shell has a narrow API and does not hide mode-specific toolbar/content logic. Existing layout tests still pass, with new coverage if the extracted helper owns meaningful behavior.

title: Expose assignee and labels in inline bead creation
description: The old new-bead dialog accepted assignee and labels through IssueDraft, and bd create still supports those fields. Decide whether inline creation should preserve that parity, then add compact controls in the normal creation flow if it should.
acceptance_criteria: Product decision is documented in the bead notes or implementation. If implemented, assignee and labels can be set before create and are passed through bd create. Existing detail editing remains consistent with the chosen model.
